On this page

Internet & Web — Quick Summary ​

No prose. Tables and key facts for Internet Technologies.

Internet vs WWW ​

InternetWorld Wide Web
WhatGlobal network of computersService running on internet
ProtocolTCP/IPHTTP / HTTPS
ContentEmail, FTP, VoIP, gaming, webWeb pages
Invented1969 (ARPANET)1991 (Tim Berners-Lee)

URL Structure ​

https://www.school.co.za:443/it/notes.html?topic=binary#section1
│       │                 │   │    │         │             │
protocol domain          port path file     query         anchor
1
2
3

Domain Name Structure ​

www  .  school  .  co  .  za
│           │       │      │
subdomain  domain  2ndTLD  TLD
1
2
3

Common TLDs:

TLDUse
.comCommercial
.orgNon-profit
.govGovernment
.edu / .acEducation
.co.zaSouth African commercial
.zaSouth Africa

Web Evolution ​

Web 1.0Web 2.0Web 3.0
Era1990s–2000s2004–nowEmerging
TypeRead onlyRead-WriteSemantic/AI
ContentOwner-createdUser-generatedMachine + user
ExamplesBrochure sitesFacebook, YouTubeAI assistants, IoT
TechStatic HTMLAJAX, APIs, socialBlockchain, AI

Static vs Dynamic Websites ​

StaticDynamic
ContentFixed HTMLGenerated per request
DatabaseNoYes
Same for all users?YesNo — personalised
TechHTML onlyHTML + PHP/Python + DB
ExampleSimple info siteOnline banking, social media

Cloud Computing ​

ModelWhat you getExample
IaaSVirtual hardwareAWS, Azure
PaaSDevelopment platformGoogle App Engine
SaaSSoftware via internetMS 365, Netflix, Gmail

Cloud advantages: anywhere access, auto backup, scalable, no hardware to buy
Cloud disadvantages: internet dependency, privacy concerns, ongoing cost, vendor lock-in

Internet Services ​

ServiceProtocolPurpose
Web browsingHTTP/HTTPSView web pages
Email (send)SMTPSend messages
Email (receive)POP3 / IMAPDownload/access messages
File transferFTP / SFTPTransfer files
Video callsVoIPReal-time voice/video
SearchHTTPFind web content

Intranet / Extranet / Internet ​

Who accessesExample
IntranetInternal employees onlySchool staff portal
ExtranetAuthorised external partiesSupplier ordering system
InternetAnyonewww

Website Evaluation Criteria ​

CriterionWhat to check
AuthorityWho wrote it? Qualifications?
AccuracyIs information correct? Referenced?
CurrencyWhen was it last updated?
PurposeInform, sell, persuade, entertain?
ObjectivityIs there bias?

HTML Basics ​

TagPurpose
<html>Root element
<head>Metadata (title, links)
<body>Visible content
<h1>–<h6>Headings
<p>Paragraph
<a href="">Hyperlink
<img src="">Image
<table>Table
<form>Input form
<input>Form field
<div>Block container
<span>Inline container

Online Safety ​

ThreatDescription
PhishingFake site/email to steal credentials
Identity theftUsing personal info for fraud
CyberbullyingHarassment via technology
Malware downloadInfected files from unsafe sites
Unsecured Wi-FiData intercepted on public networks

Protection: HTTPS, 2FA, VPN on public Wi-Fi, don't overshare, privacy settings on social media

Cookies ​

  • Small text files stored by browser
  • Track visits, preferences, login sessions
  • Can be used for targeted advertising
  • User can clear cookies or block them
  • GDPR/POPIA requires consent notification
Pager
